title: Warm Peppery Korean Chicken Naengmyeon
description: A peppery and springy Korean dish featuring chicken breasts, served with a cold noodle dish called naengmyeon, cooked in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot, this recipe is ovo-vegetarian friendly.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the naengmyeon noodles according to the package instructions.
In a large bowl, whisk together the gochugaru, soy sauce, sesame oil, and minced garlic to make the sauce.
Slice the cooked chicken breast into thin strips and add to the bowl with the sauce, tossing to coat.
In a ttukbaegi earthenware pot, combine the cooked noodles, chicken, and sauce, and stir to combine.
Top with chopped green onions and a fried egg.
Garnish with additional green onions and gochugaru if desired.
Cook the chicken breast in the oven at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 20 minutes, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 165 degrees Fahrenheit.
